I’ll follow the Multica workflow first: read the issue, metadata, and full comment history, then move it to in progress before touching code.Using `multica-working-on-issues` for the required issue workflow, plus `ariadne-multica-reference-lens` and `ariadne-local-first-guardrails` because this is Ariadne parity/product-shape work with explicit local-first constraints.The issue is explicitly read-only: I’ll inspect the references and produce the architecture/build-issue proposal as a Multica comment, with no file edits, commits, pushes, or PRs.Metadata is empty. The only prior comments are runtime startup failures, so there’s no substantive product guidance beyond the issue body.I’m checking out the two referenced repositories now so the proposal is grounded in their actual structure rather than README memory.Both checkouts are still running; I’ll wait for them and then scan README/config/runtime files first.The checkouts succeeded. I’m scanning the minimal agent surfaces now: entrypoints, README claims, configuration shape, tool/runtime loop, and tests.The two references point in different directions: mini-swe-agent optimizes for the smallest reliable bash-only loop; cc-mini adds product scaffolding around an interactive harness. I’m reading the core loops and permission/session pieces to separate v0.1 essentials from later features.I’ve got enough to outline the core loop from mini-swe-agent. I’m now checking cc-mini’s harness pieces: explicit tools, permission gates, session persistence, planning/coordinator, and sandboxing so the proposed v0.1 does not overreach.Ariadne’s v0.1 should probably take mini-swe-agent’s loop discipline and cc-mini’s explicit permission/session affordances, but keep them ticket-centered and local. I’m checking the exact tool list and sandbox logic before drafting the result.Before I write the final comment, I’m doing a quick read-only check of Ariadne’s own docs so the proposal fits the existing Ticket-centered vocabulary instead of only describing a standalone CLI agent.Ariadne has local uncommitted changes in unrelated files; I’m leaving them alone and only reading docs.The Ariadne docs confirm the main boundary: this should be a BuildTicket / TicketAssignment / AgentRun loop, not a new goal-first planner or hosted agent product. I’m turning that into a concise issue comment with three backlog-ready build issues.I have the proposal ready. I’ll post it as the issue result comment now, then move the issue to `in_review` per the runtime workflow.Posted the required Multica issue comment for `LOC-109` with the v0.1 architecture proposal, reference takeaways, three draft build issues, and blockers/missing resources.

I did not edit files, commit, push, create a PR, or create child issues. The issue is now `in_review`.
